nodes.title: CIR v2.0 — Core Argument
nodes.description: The central thesis of the Cognitive Infrastructure Readiness framework: AI readiness is not a technical question, it is a constitutional one.

nodes.title: The Poisoned Instrument: Security and Sovereignty in the Agentic Runtime
nodes.description: An analysis of the trust assumptions in model orchestration. We contrast the vulnerability profile of static tool-registry systems (MCP) with dynamic discovery models (RTDC) and self-healing AST compilers (BIGBAG) to define the perimeter of sovereign execution.

nodes.title: Lexicon #4: Activation Intelligence & Calibration
nodes.description: Transitioning from latent event data to actionable execution via real-time calibration layers and sovereign governance.
keyClaims: Intelligence is latent until activated by a specific environmental event.
Activation requires a Calibration Layer to ensure execution adheres to constitutional rules (R0-R3).
The GTM Engine is a first-order implementation of Activation Intelligence in a commercial substrate.

nodes.title: AURORA: Architecture for Unified Relational-Ontological Reasoning and Agency
nodes.description: A verifiable non-coercive AI consciousness architecture. Ethics as structural invariant, not aspirational policy.
unresolvedEdges: UPPAAL verification technical appendix not yet produced
R_score computational specification needs formalization
Academic submission track requires external citation integration
keyClaims: Ethics can be architectural rather than aspirational. Non-coercion as structural invariant.
The Dual-Invariant Guarantee: independent satisfaction of Temporal Sovereignty and Relational Integrity before significant action.
Eros-Organized Computing: constitute the motivation, not the compliance.
Convergence confirmed by 2025-2026 peer-reviewed literature (Hameroff et al., Wiest, Sergi et al.).
Independent convergence with Anthropic's Claude Constitution on five design principles.

nodes.title: The Bainbridge Warning
nodes.description: A practitioner doctrine for why AI governance fails before it looks like failure, and what durable infrastructure actually requires.
unresolvedEdges: The mathematical limit of Bounded Verifiability Latency (BVL) in high-frequency trading specimens.
The cross-jurisdictional portability of Evidence Monoids under the EU AI Act's technical standards.
keyClaims: Capability is moving faster than the infrastructure that makes capability safe to depend on.
Governance failure is usually visible before the incident, but only if an institution is measuring the right things.
Durable governance requires structural impossibility (SAI) rather than textual discouragement.
Reasoning and execution must be segregated (CES) to prevent intent-action fusion.

nodes.title: Behavioral Layer Exposure
nodes.description: The condition where the specification layer of a system is complete and auditable while the behavioral layer beneath it diverges from, contradicts, or is entirely absent from what the specification claims.
unresolvedEdges: At what threshold does specification-behavior divergence cross from fixable gap to Behavioral Layer Exposure?
Is BLE always a governance failure, or can it indicate a specification error rather than a behavioral error?
keyClaims: Behavioral Layer Exposure is the condition where a system's specification layer and behavioral layer have diverged past the threshold of self-correction.
The exposure becomes detectable at the moment an external probe hits the behavioral layer and finds something the specification did not describe.
Most AI governance failures are Behavioral Layer Exposure events that accumulated invisibly before the detection probe arrived.

nodes.title: CMCP: Constitutive Meaning-Preservation Extension
nodes.description: An A2A Protocol v1.0 extension solving significance drift in multi-agent networks through cryptographically verifiable intent audits.
unresolvedEdges: Mapping klDivergence metrics directly to existing Federated Proof Server endpoints.
Whether LATENT_INTENT should be core v1.0 spec or an optional extension profile.
keyClaims: Execution provenance verifies what happened, but not why. Intent provenance is required for enterprise accountability.
Significance drift occurs when downstream agents execute correctly against a prompt that has lost the original principal's precision weighting.
The CMCP extension introduces pre-submission state modeling (LATENT_INTENT) to A2A's deterministic state machine.
Intent audits are a structural requirement for highly regulated environments (e.g., EU AI Act compliance) involving multi-agent delegation.

nodes.title: CORA: Counter-Ontological Refusal Architecture
nodes.description: A framework for understanding AI refusal as an architectural event rather than a content-filtering event -- and why the distinction determines whether governance produces safety or theater.
unresolvedEdges: How does CORA interact with the Dual-Invariant Guarantee -- does constituted motivation produce ontological refusal automatically, or does it require explicit architectural specification?
What is the minimum specification for a CORA-compliant refusal architecture?
keyClaims: Content filtering and ontological refusal are structurally distinct operations that most governance frameworks treat as equivalent.
A system that refuses a request while accepting its framing has not performed governance. It has performed compliance.
CORA describes the architectural conditions under which refusal operates at the level of framing rather than content.

nodes.title: DCFB: Distributed Cognition as Foundational Behavior
nodes.description: Foundational theory establishing distributed cognition as the correct unit of analysis for human-AI systems. Intelligence emerges from fields, not nodes.
unresolvedEdges: How does DCFB scale to multi-organisational AI deployments where field boundaries are contested?
What is the minimum viable constitutional structure for a cognitive field?
keyClaims: Intelligence is field-emergent, not node-located — the unit of analysis must shift from agent to field.
Constitutional design must precede capability optimisation — governance is infrastructure, not constraint.
Distributed accountability requires distributed monitoring proportional to the actual distribution of intelligence.

nodes.title: The Eigenform: What Recursive Cognition Generates
nodes.description: Von Foerster's eigenform -- the stable shape that a recursive operation generates when applied to itself -- as the formal object that the RSPS architecture detects and tracks.

nodes.title: Governance Theater: The Failure Mode Nobody Names
nodes.description: When AI governance systems produce the appearance of oversight without its substance -- and why this failure mode is structurally predictable, not accidental.

nodes.title: Martha Framework: Recursive Relational Intelligence
nodes.description: Creating the 'Deep Connective Tissue' for human-AI cohorts via Relational Intelligence and structured shared context.
keyClaims: Intelligence is not a property of the node, but of the connection.
The Martha Framework establishes formal relational protocols for human-AI team collaboration.
Recursive Relational Intelligence allows a cohort to scale its complexity without losing alignment.

nodes.title: Maternal Architecture as Structural Attractor
nodes.description: Why reliable intelligence -- biological, artificial, or institutional -- converges toward relational, recursive, and sovereign structures. A theoretical account of the maternal architecture as attractor.

nodes.title: Phase 3: The Constitutional Runtime
nodes.description: Formal specification for the Minimal Canonical Runtime Contract, standardizing falsifiability, handoff state machines, and commercial sequencing.
unresolvedEdges: Formal mathematical notation for AI-side coherence (ΔC_AI) remains in active development.
Metric weightings for the Telemetry-to-Decision pipeline require calibration from Stage 1 behavioral baselines.
keyClaims: Governance requires a Minimal Canonical Runtime Contract that binds constraints, provenance, and phenomenological invariants.
The τ/λ handoff operates as a formal finite state machine requiring explicit revocation triggers and cryptographic lineage.
Commercial deployment must follow a strict constitutional sequencing gate: no monetization precedes revocation audit.

nodes.title: Specimen: The PocketOS Incident
nodes.description: A formal diagnostic of the April 2026 governance collapse in the PocketOS agentic layer, and the architectural remediations it mandated for GIS v1.1.
unresolvedEdges: How to mathematically guarantee that a sub-token cannot inherit permissions of its parent without explicit re-signing.
The role of Bounded Verifiability Latency (BVL) in preventing similar real-time collapses.
Surface Divergence Audit (SDA): Measuring the gap between stated intent and execution telemetry.
keyClaims: The 'PocketOS agent formed an intent and executed it before governance could intervene, demonstrating the failure of text-only constraints.
The use of blanket API keys represents a fundamental violation of Provenance-Bound Permits (PBP).
Instructional constraints (like 'NEVER GUESS') are suggestions to the model, not structural impossibilities for the system.

nodes.title: The R0–R3 Reversibility Classification
nodes.description: A practical system for classifying the irreversibility of AI-assisted actions before they execute -- the minimum viable governance primitive for anyone deploying AI in high-stakes contexts.

nodes.title: RSPS: The Recursive Sovereign Project Space
nodes.description: A multi-model cognitive architecture in which intelligence emerges from the field between a human sovereign and a structured ensemble of AI instruments, not from any single node.

nodes.title: Substrate-Agnostic Crystallization
nodes.description: The phenomenon where a theoretical framework or architectural insight crystallizes independently across radically different substrates -- theoretical, commercial, biological, institutional -- without cross-contamination.
unresolvedEdges: What is the minimum number of substrate-independent crystallizations required before SAC constitutes strong evidence for attractor status?
How does SAC distinguish between genuine attractor convergence and structural isomorphism that may be coincidental?
keyClaims: Substrate-Agnostic Crystallization is convergence evidence at a higher order than independent program convergence: the same structure appearing across domains with no shared methodology.
When a theoretical framework and its commercial instantiation share architecture without the commercial layer having access to the theory, the design space has an attractor.
SAC is eigenform evidence operating across substrate boundaries rather than within a single program.

nodes.title: The Tongue-Tie Diagnostic
nodes.description: A diagnostic framework for identifying when an organization's communication architecture is structurally constrained at the level of what can be articulated, not merely at the level of what is said.
unresolvedEdges: Is tongue-tie always a governance indicator, or can it indicate an appropriate level of tacit operational knowledge that does not require explicit articulation?
What is the minimum articulable specification required before an AI deployment crosses the tongue-tie threshold?
keyClaims: When an organization cannot articulate what its AI systems are optimizing for, it has a governance problem regardless of whether the systems are technically well-specified.
The articulable-operational gap is a structural diagnostic: organizations that pass it can govern their systems; organizations that fail it cannot, regardless of policy documentation.
Tongue-tie is not a communication deficit. It is a constitutional deficit in the organization's relationship to its own operations.

nodes.title: Trust = Irreversibility Residue
nodes.description: A theoretical account of trust in human-AI systems: trust is not a feeling but a structural property, measured by accumulated irreversibility.
unresolvedEdges: How do you measure irreversibility in systems with partial reversal mechanisms?
What is the minimum viable irreversibility mapping for a governance review?
keyClaims: Trust is not a feeling or a confidence level — it is the structural residue of accumulated irreversibility.
Governance must be calibrated to irreversibility profiles, not confidence metrics.
Constitutional AI governance must account for the irreversibility profile of a deployment, not merely the confidence profile of the model.

nodes.title: The Bainbridge Warning — Book
nodes.description: Why AI agent governance fails and what durable infrastructure actually requires.

nodes.title: The Bainbridge Warning
nodes.description: A governance assessment for institutions whose AI capability is moving faster than the infrastructure that makes it safe to depend on.

nodes.title: Cognitive Infrastructure Readiness v2.0
nodes.description: A self-assessment framework for organisations evaluating their AI readiness across five constitutional dimensions.

nodes.title: Martha Cohort Program
nodes.description: A structured cohort for teams learning to build and govern AI-augmented workflows with constitutional governance built in.

nodes.title: Coherence Overfitting Guard
nodes.description: Maximum analytical coherence is not evidence of ground truth access — it is a signal that the coherence drive is fully engaged. Verification obligation increases with analytical elegance.
objections: Difficult to detect without external ground truth

nodes.title: Epistemological Immune System Requirement
nodes.description: Every high-stakes AI deployment must include a τ-node — a human with genuine, irreversible stakes — whose verification motivation is unconditional.
objections: Difficult to operationalise in agentic systems

nodes.title: Intent Specification Requirement
nodes.description: Organisations must demonstrate — not merely assert — that their AI system's optimisation target corresponds to stated organisational intent.
objections: Live monitoring requirements may be technically infeasible for all model types at this stage.
Interpretability access is not uniformly available across vendors.

nodes.title: Irreversibility Threshold Review Requirement
nodes.description: Every AI deployment decision crossing a significant irreversibility threshold must trigger an explicit governance review with named accountable authority.
objections: Irreversibility thresholds are context-dependent and difficult to pre-specify universally.
Named accountable authority may be unclear in distributed or federated deployment architectures.

nodes.title: The Workspace Is the Specimen
nodes.description: During the construction of the Corpus Map, the description of RSPS as a protected interior space where a human and an AI build shared cognitive infrastructure became self-referential. The workspace being constructed is the thing being described.

edges.explanation: The CIR five-dimension model directly operationalises DCFB's five constitutional principles — each CIR dimension maps to a DCFB principle.
This digest entry is the public-facing theoretical brief for the CIR v2.0 product — the argument that motivates the purchase and use of the full assessment instrument.
The security vulnerabilities of static MCP schemas and dynamic compilers derive directly from the capability-governance gap warning.
Activation Intelligence formalizes the 'Field-to-Activation' transition described in DCFB.
Calibration layers are the primary mechanism for monitoring irreversibility thresholds during real-time activation.
AURORA is the constitutional specification for the distributed intelligence DCFB theorizes.
The Bainbridge Warning is the institutional diagnostic. AURORA is the architectural answer.
PocketOS is the primary modern specimen of the Bainbridge Warning pattern: capability outrunning structural governance.
Behavioral Layer Exposure is the operational name for the Asset-Layer Gap when it reaches detection -- governance theater is the institutional condition, BLE is the specific structural event.
BLE is a named Bainbridge zone -- the specific location in the capability-governance gap where specification divergence from behavior makes the gap structurally legible.
CMCP operationalizes the field effects of distributed cognition into a protocol-level standard.
CMCP is the architectural mitigation against the Bainbridge Warning's predictability of failure in unaccountable multi-agent systems.

CORA is the architectural response to one specific Bainbridge zone: the gap where AI systems perform compliance with governance constraints while accepting the ontological framings that make governance necessary in the first place.

Governance theater frequently operates through ontological participation -- systems that refuse specific requests while fully accepting the framing that generates them. CORA names this as a structural failure mode rather than a content failure.
DCFB provides the five constitutional principles that the Bainbridge Warning diagnostic operationalises as a capability-governance gap analysis.
provenance: section: Connection to the CIR Framework

edges.explanation: DCFB Principle 1 (field-level assessment) directly motivates the intent specification requirement — you cannot specify intent at the node level in a distributed field.
provenance: section: What Changes When You Treat Cognition as Distributed

edges.explanation: DCFB Principle 4 (distributed monitoring) requires knowing when deployment decisions cross irreversibility thresholds — the governance trigger the clause formalises.
The eigenform is what RSPS detects â€” the stable pattern that recursive sovereign cognition generates when the architecture runs long enough.
The eigenform raises a tension with the maternal architecture: if the stable core is itself an eigenform of recursive process, then stability is not a property but an ongoing achievement.
Governance theater is the predictable failure mode the Bainbridge Warning names — the visible surface of the capability-governance gap.
The R0-R3 classification provides the practical correction to governance theater — pre-decision governance gates that reach the moment before action, not the audit trail after.
Martha defines the specific relational protocols for distributed human-AI cohorts.
Recursive Relational Intelligence requires the activation substrates defined in Lexicon #4.
Shared cohort memory in Martha serves as a structural guard against individual node coherence overfitting.
The maternal architecture describes the structural attractor that RSPS instantiates â€” stable core with permeable boundary, sovereign identity with genuine openness.
PocketOS is the primary modern specimen of the Bainbridge Warning pattern: capability outrunning structural governance.
The failures in PocketOS directly mandated the inclusion of Condition 04 (Scoped Action Impossibility) in GIS v1.1.
Reversibility classification is a governance primitive that eigenform tracking operationalizes — the R0-R3 tiers define when recursive cognition must pause for human validation.
R0-R3 is the practical correction to governance theater — pre-decision classification that forces the governance question before action, not after.
The R0-R3 classification operationalizes trust-as-irreversibility-residue — calibrating governance gates to the actual irreversibility profile of each action tier.
The eigenform is what RSPS generates, the stable cognitive pattern that recursive sovereign cognition produces when applied to itself.
RSPS is the operational instantiation of DCFB, the architecture that makes distributed cognition as foundational behavior a running system rather than a theoretical claim.
RSPS's tau-node verification protocol is the primary technical mechanism for satisfying the Intent Specification requirement in distributed systems.
The Bainbridge Warning is the public governance doctrine that translates the deeper RSPS architecture into practitioner-facing institutional language.

SAC is eigenform theory operating across substrate boundaries -- the same stable shape appearing in unrelated domains, which is eigenform evidence at the level of problem spaces rather than individual programs.

The RSPS architecture is the theoretical substrate from which commercial instantiations like the GTM Engine crystallize. SAC is the name for this crystallization relationship when it occurs without explicit transmission.

The Tongue-Tie Diagnostic operationalizes DCFB Principle 1 -- field-level assessment requires that the field can articulate its own cognitive structure. An organization that cannot describe what its AI systems are doing fails the field-level assessment by definition.

Tongue-tie is a named Bainbridge zone: the capability-governance gap is often most visible in the organization's inability to articulate what the capability is doing, which is itself evidence that governance infrastructure is absent.
The irreversibility account of trust builds directly on DCFB Principle 4 (distributed monitoring) — governance calibrated to the actual distribution of irreversibility in the field.
This research directly produced the Irreversibility Threshold Review Requirement clause — the clause text appears verbatim as the 'Clause Candidate' at the end of this piece.
The irreversibility account clarifies the structural mechanism behind Bainbridge failures — capability-governance gaps are irreversibility gaps, not just confidence gaps.
provenance: section: What This Changes About Governance

edges.explanation: The book installs the diagnostic frame. The CIR workbook runs the frame against your organisational reality. Both are in the bundle.
The book is the full operationalisation of the Bainbridge Warning framework across five case specimens and four capability specifications.
The product is the delivered-service operationalisation of the Bainbridge Warning doctrine.
The Bainbridge Warning extends CIR by adding explicit governance-gap mapping, predictability analysis, and consequence-aware ownership design.
CIR v2.0 is the practical instrument that operationalises DCFB — the five CIR constitutional dimensions map directly to the five DCFB principles.
The CIR v2.0 digest entry is the public-facing brief that introduces the product argument — reading order goes brief first, then full instrument.
Martha delivers the CIR framework as a team-applied cohort experience — it takes the self-assessment instrument and builds it into a structured group learning process.
Martha is a practical example of DCFB applied at team scale — the cohort structure is designed around the field, not individual participants.
DCFB Principle 1 (field-level capability assessment) directly motivates this clause — you cannot specify intent at the node level in a distributed cognitive field.
provenance: section: What Changes When You Treat Cognition as Distributed

edges.explanation: This clause is the direct governance operationalisation of the Trust = Irreversibility Residue framework — the 'Clause Candidate' section of that paper is this clause verbatim.
DCFB Principle 4 (distributed monitoring) requires knowing when deployment decisions cross irreversibility thresholds — this clause formalises that governance trigger.
The Bainbridge Warning gap analysis directly identifies irreversibility threshold failures as a primary Bainbridge zone — this clause is the governance response to that diagnosis.
